title: CSS Animations
content:
### CSS Animations

**Animations** create complex, multi-step motion without JavaScript.

---

### Animation vs Transition

| Transition | Animation |
|-----------|-----------|
| Needs a trigger (hover, click) | Runs automatically |
| Two states: start -> end | Multiple keyframes |
| Simple, limited | Complex, looping |
| One-time per trigger | Can repeat infinitely |

---

### Animation Properties

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `animation-name` | Name of the `@keyframes` rule |
| `animation-duration` | How long one cycle takes |
| `animation-timing-function` | Speed curve |
| `animation-delay` | Delay before start |
| `animation-iteration-count` | Number of repeats |
| `animation-direction` | Normal, reverse, alternate |
| `animation-fill-mode` | Styles before/after |
| `animation-play-state` | Pause or running |
| `animation` | Shorthand for all |

---

### Animation Shorthand

```css
.element {
  animation: name duration timing-function delay iteration-count direction fill-mode;
}

/* Example */
.element {
  animation: fadeIn 0.5s ease 0.2s 1 normal forwards;
}

/* Minimum (name + duration) */
.element {
  animation: slideIn 1s;
}
```

---

### Key Properties Explained

```css
/* Duration */
animation-duration: 2s;      /* 2 seconds */
animation-duration: 500ms;   /* 500 milliseconds */

/* Iteration */
animation-iteration-count: 1;      /* Once (default) */
animation-iteration-count: 3;      /* Three times */
animation-iteration-count: infinite; /* Loop forever */

/* Direction */
animation-direction: normal;    /* Forward (default) */
animation-direction: reverse;   /* Backward */
animation-direction: alternate; /* Forward then backward */
animation-direction: alternate-reverse; /* Backward then forward */

/* Fill mode */
animation-fill-mode: none;     /* Default */
animation-fill-mode: forwards; /* Keep end state */
animation-fill-mode: backwards;/* Apply first frame before delay */
animation-fill-mode: both;     /* Both forwards and backwards */

/* Play state */
animation-play-state: running; /* Playing (default) */
animation-play-state: paused;  /* Paused */
```

---

### Multiple Animations

```css
.element {
  animation: 
    fadeIn 1s ease,
    slideUp 0.5s ease 0.3s;
}
```

> **Best Practice:** Use `animation-fill-mode: forwards` to keep the final state after animation ends.
codeExample:
Fade In
Bounce

quiz.question: Which animation-fill-mode keeps the end state after animation?
